title: Lycianthes sprucei
intro: Lycianthes sprucei, also known as the Brazilian blueberry or frambosa, is a perennial shrub native to the subtropical regions of South America, belonging to the Solanaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lycianthes sprucei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lycianthes sprucei is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations.
